steps.title: Find the two independent clauses
steps.content: Before the blank: “her work was featured at the Alex Schlesinger Gallery in [location]”—an independent clause with subject (her work) and verb (was featured). After the blank: “the exhibited photographs, with their vivid pastel colors, overexposed tones, and mirrorlike symmetry, captivated audiences”—another independent clause with subject (the photographs and modifiers) and verb (captivated).

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content: A semicolon joins two independent clauses when there's no coordinating conjunction. Choice A correctly applies this rule. The other options fail to properly join these clauses. This matches choice A — A is correct.
